moves.note: Add top quality name, strong scanner

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Trim QQQ to 15% as indicated by the scanner.
Initiate a new position in AVGO at 5% for quality exposure.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a realistic target of ~1.5%/month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, prioritizing stability. Expect lower volatility, but also more modest returns.

moves.note: Add quality tech, strong scanner setup
Add quality tech, strong scanner setup

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF risk.
Trim QQQ to 10% as flagged by the scanner.
Initiate a new position in AVGO at 8% for quality tech exposure.
Initiate a new position in AAPL at 7% for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~10% for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and growth, with a focus on managing downside risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain some leveraged exposure.
Sell SPAL due to being below stop and poor performance.
Trim QQQ to 10% as flagged by the scanner.
Initiate new positions in AVGO (10%), GUSH (8%), and AAPL (7%).
Focus on highest-scoring scanner setups for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into high-conviction setups and some leverage. It carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the decay inherent in leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage this elevated risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift to income.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize monthly distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta sector and single name risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index funds. It prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
